subject: Driver card not activating - Jordan Wells
description: New driver Jordan Wells (Driver #47) unable to activate Coast card. SMS verification failing repeatedly. Driver is at a truck stop in Birmingham, AL and needs to fuel up.
resolution: Mismatched last 4 of SSN in system - data entry error during card issuance. Corrected SSN and card activated successfully within 2 hours.
notes: Mike expressed minor frustration about data entry QA but appreciated fast resolution. Consider adding SSN validation check during card issuance flow.

subject: Bulk card order - 5 new drivers
description: Request for 5 new driver cards for recently hired drivers. Standard issuance request.
resolution: 5 cards processed and shipped. All activated successfully within 48 hours of receipt.
notes: Smooth process. Mike happy with turnaround time.

subject: Request to add 6 new driver cards
assigned_to: CS Team - Maria Santos → Escalated to Risk
description: Raj Patel requested 6 additional driver cards, which would bring total active cards to 24 - well exceeding the stated fleet size of 12 vehicles. Request flagged by CS team and escalated to risk for review.
notes: CS team correctly identified the discrepancy and escalated. Raj was upset about the hold and demanded to speak with a manager. James Ortiz handling relationship. Cards blocked per Delia's directive pending account review.

subject: Transaction declined - insufficient limit
description: Raj called upset that a $2,400 transaction at a truck parts store was declined. Account was near credit limit. Demanded immediate line increase.
resolution: Explained that account is at 68% utilization and approaching limit. Advised on payment timing to free up available credit. Raj was not satisfied and requested formal line increase review.
notes: Raj was aggressive on the call. Pattern of pushing for higher limits. Forwarded to James Ortiz for relationship management.

subject: API documentation request for fleet management integration
assigned_to: CS Team - Alex Chen → Escalated to Engineering
description: Greenfield's IT team wants API documentation to build a custom integration between Coast transaction data and their proprietary fleet management platform. Requesting real-time transaction feeds, fuel consumption data, and driver activity logs via REST API.
notes: Engineering team reviewing API access scope. This is a key request for our largest enterprise accounts. Product team involved in scoping.

subject: Data export for annual audit
description: Linda requested full transaction data export for calendar year 2024 for their annual audit. Needs CSV format with merchant names, amounts, dates, driver IDs, and receipt status.
resolution: Generated and delivered 2024 transaction report (14,847 transactions). Linda confirmed receipt and format was acceptable.
notes: Smooth process. Linda mentioned this was faster than their previous fuel card provider.

subject: BuildOps transaction sync error
description: Several transactions from the past week not syncing to BuildOps. Dave reports missing fuel transactions for 4 drivers. BuildOps dashboard showing 'sync pending' for 72+ hours.
notes: Investigating potential API rate limit issue with BuildOps. May need to coordinate with BuildOps support team.

subject: How to add spending controls per driver
description: Carlos called asking how to set different spending limits for each driver. Wants installers limited to fuel and Home Depot only, but wants his own card unrestricted.
resolution: Walked Carlos through spending control settings in the Coast portal. Set up custom rules: 3 installer cards limited to fuel + hardware stores, Carlos's card unrestricted. Carlos seemed pleased with the granularity.
notes: Good sign for activation - Carlos is engaging with the product more. Follow up in a week to see if the other drivers have started using cards.
